tasks.text: Profile and optimize IntersectBVH - the top hotspot at 42.3% inclusive
Optimize IntersectBVH traversal: Reduce branch mispredictions by reordering stack push to put closer child first more efficiently
Optimize rayTriangle: Use SoA triangle data layout or precompute edge vectors to reduce memory traffic in leaf intersection
Reduce stack pointer arithmetic in BVH traversal: Use iterative traversal with less push/pop overhead
Precompute edge vectors in triangle data: Replace (v1,v2,v3) storage with (v0,e1,e2) to eliminate 3×3 subtractions per rayTriangle call, saving ~19% exclusive hotspot cost
Apply edge-precomputation: 1) Change Object struct 2) Update rayTriangle 3) Update all allocation/destruction/creation code 4) Update UV calculation functions
Fix load/loadObj.c: store v0/e1/e2 instead of v1/v2/v3 (precompute edges) - 3 errors
Fix object/scene.c: use v0/e1/e2 instead of v1/v2/v3 - 6 errors
Fix render/cpu/ray.c: use v0/e1/e2 with precomputed edge reconstruction where needed - 6 errors
Optimize IntersectBVH traversal: reorder stack push to put closer child first to reduce BVH traversal steps and branch mispredictions
Optimize IntersectBVH_Shadow traversal: apply same closer-child-first optimization for shadow rays

notes.text: Baseline bench: avg 11.006ms, median 10.467ms, p99 14.904ms at 1080x720. Top hotspots: IntersectBVH (42.3%), rayTriangle (19.3%), rayAABB_inv (9.0%), rayCollision (8.5%), SampleEmission (7.1%), rayAABB_inv_x2_soa (6.6%), IntersectBVH_Shadow (5.7%), sampleFace (5.4%), RayBoxItersect (4.8%), RayCast (4.5%)
Need flame graph data for line-level annotation. Running makeFlame() to get perf.data.

Analyzed perf data: IntersectBVH 41.7% inclusive, rayTriangle 19.2% exclusive (inlined inside IntersectBVH), rayAABB_inv 9.0%, rayAABB_inv_x2_soa 6.8%, IntersectBVH_Shadow 5.9%. Key insight: rayTriangle is the dominant leaf cost at 19.2%.
The BVH traversal uses a stack-based approach with push/pop. The stack operations and bounds checking are in rayAABB_inv_x2_soa (6.8%). The triangle intersection is in rayTriangle (19.2%).

Starting optimization: Precompute and store triangle edges (v0, e1, e2) instead of (v1, v2, v3) to save 3 subtractions and improve cache locality in rayTriangle. This directly targets 19.2% exclusive cost.

Planning the edge-precomputation optimization: Changing Object struct to store v0, e1, e2 instead of v1, v2, v3. This affects: object.h (struct), object.c (rayTriangle, IntersectBVH, CreateObjectBVH, etc.), load/loadObj.c (LoadObj), CreateCube, Object_Destroy, CalculateFaceEmissions, sampleFace usage. Will add new float3* arrays: v0, e1, e2 replacing v1, v2, v3.

Beginning edge-precomputation optimization. Changing Object struct: store v0, e1, e2 instead of v1, v2, v3. This eliminates 3x3=9 subtractions per rayTriangle call (19.2% of total time). Also need to recompute v1=v0+e1 and v2=v0+e2 for UV calculation functions.

The codebase is in a partially-converted state: object.c uses v0/e1/e2 (CreateCube, rayTriangle, IntersectBVH, etc.) but object.h still declares v1/v2/v3, and render.c still references obj->v1/v2/v3. The struct needs to be changed to v0/e1/e2, and render.c needs updating to reconstruct v1=v0+e1, v2=v0+e2 from the precomputed edges. Also need to add uv handling in render.c.
Applied object.h struct change: v1/v2/v3 -> v0/e1/e2. Now need to update render.c.
Build failed: load/loadObj.c, object/scene.c, and render/cpu/ray.c still reference old v1/v2/v3. Need to read these files and convert them to use v0/e1/e2.
Reading load/loadObj.c lines around 90-100 to understand vertex creation pattern
Build succeeded after applying v0/e1/e2 precomputation across all files. Running benchmark to measure performance impact.
Edge precomputation completed with +1.7% avg, +2.4% median improvement. Next target: BVH traversal optimization - looking at stack-based traversal in IntersectBVH (still ~40% of time).

Need to read the full IntersectBVH function in object.c to understand the stack-based traversal pattern and identify optimization opportunities. Also need to check if closer-child-first ordering is applied.
Looking at IntersectBVH function in object.c to understand stack traversal pattern and plan closer-child-first optimization

The IntersectBVH and IntersectBVH_Shadow both use a stack-based traversal. Currently they push children regardless of order. The closer-child-first heuristic pushes the nearer child last so it's popped first, reducing traversal steps. Need to read the full functions.

IntersectBVH (primary) already has closer-child-first ordering at lines 584-590. IntersectBVH_Shadow (lines 636-638) does NOT — it pushes children in fixed order. Adding distance-based ordering there should reduce traversal depth for shadow rays, targeting the 5.9% inclusive cost.
